revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 128x32 pixel-art sprite sheet, scaled up for preview but preserving crisp square pixels. The actual sheet content must be exactly 128 px wide by 32 px tall, arranged as 1 row x 4 equal 32x32 px cells. Solid #FF00FF chroma-key background fills every empty pixel. No gradients in the background.

Style/profile: SNES 16-bit platformer pixel art, crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur, consistent palette, readable pixel clusters, top-left light source, one-pixel dark outline where useful.

Asset: original animated enemy sprite sheet, compact readable idle/hop loop. Subject is a cute mischievous karakasa-obake: an old mythical-Japan oiled-paper umbrella yokai, vermilion-and-white canopy accents, muted matcha and moss green satoyama palette details, one big sleepy eye, playful long tongue, one sandal-wearing leg, thin wisps of dark purple impurity haze clinging close to the umbrella hem. Cute and playful, not scary, bright cheerful palette.

Frame layout, exactly one row of four equal cells:
- Frame 1, cell 1: crouched on its single leg, preparing to hop.
- Frame 2, cell 2: springing upward, umbrella canopy slightly squashed.
- Frame 3, cell 3: airborne, canopy stretched open.
- Frame 4, cell 4: landing, canopy fluttering, loops back to frame 1.

Identity and scale lock: every frame must depict the exact same character at the same scale. Keep head/eye size, canopy diameter, head-to-body ratio, body width, leg thickness, sandal size, tongue size, and outline thickness identical across frames. Only re-pose the same character; do not redraw, resize, restyle, or change proportions. Upright body height must match across upright frames. Every frame stays fully inside its own 32x32 cell with clear solid magenta margin from all cell edges.

Cleanliness: do not draw cell separator lines, grid lines, frame borders, frame numbers, labels, text, UI, watermarks, logos, or symbols anywhere. No detached fragments, isolated pixel islands, seam slivers, dust specks, leftover haze residue, or stray pixels. The dark purple haze must be attached close to the hem and contained inside each figure silhouette area, with no loose fragments.

Avoid completely: Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, Sakuna characters, logos, likenesses, letter emblems, samurai armor, kabuto helmet, ninja outfit, two-sword daisho, gun, modern objects, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, bird, companion creature, text, watermark, UI.
